ety: Pārsī mizd, mizhd; Pehl. mizd; Zend mizhda, fr. mis+dā = S. मस् (मस्यति) or मिष् (मेषति)+धा

ety: muzd, q.v.+ūr = war or uvar = Zend bara, rt. bar = S. भर् (भृ)

more: n. m. P. muzd-var . A laborer; carrier; porter; cooly. Naukar kisī ko rakh lo satāne ke vāste! Mazdūr ḍhūṅḍo nāz uṭhāne ke vāste! Sahar. The lover to his mistress. A servant keep whom you may just browbeat, A porter seek to bear your humours sweet. mazdūr'ī ; Pop. mazūrī ; Rus. majūrī , n. f. 1. The wages of labor. 2. Work; labor. mazdūrī k. , v. a. To work; labor. mehnat mazdūrī kar-ke peṭ pālnā , v. n. To labor for a living.
(prop. muzdūr, from مزد muzd ) s. m. A labourer, carrier, porter.
subs: s.f. A tax (formerly levied) on day-labourers.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مزدور (muzdūr, “mercenary; labourer”), which in turn inherited from Middle Persian [script needed] (myzdwbl /⁠mizdwar⁠/, “hireling, hired laborer”).

(from مضغ) s. m. 1. A lump of flesh. 2. A glutton, an ill bred person.

part. adj. & s.m. Troublesome, vexatious, hurtful, noxious, pernicious, destructive, mischievous; — molester, vexer, hurter, painer, troubler, tormentor, pesterer, oppressor, tyrant; — a miser; a wicked person; a wretch; a knave
more: adj. Noxious; hurtful; vexatious; obnoxious. mūzī , n. m. 1. A tormenter; an oppressor. 1. Mūzī ko namāz chhoṛ-ke māre . Prov. You may leave your prayers to kill a snake. 2. Qatl-al-mūzī qabl-al-īzā! Prov. Slay the serpent before he bites you! 2. (Illit. mūjī, mūṅjī ) A miser. Mile tum, saīyāṅ, mūṅjī, joṛte har dam pūṅjī, Kar tāle meṅ band, hāth meṅ rakhte kūṅjī . Play. A miser, husband, found in you, each moment hoarding still, The key in your hands ever is, all locked up in the till. 3. A wicked person; a wretch; knave. Kyoṅ be mūzī! nahīṅ māntā? mūzī-panā , n. m. 1. Noxiousness; hurtfulness. 2. Stinginess; parsimony. mūzī kā chuṅgal , The clutches of a tyrant. mūzī kā māl , A miser's property. Mūzī kā māl, nikle phūṭ-ke-khāl . Prov. The miser's wealth breaks out in sores. (It is unlucky and brings evil on his heir).

(part. act. IV. of أذي Heb. ) adj. Noxious, troublesome, importunate, vexatious, pernicious; hurter, vexer, tormenter, pesterer, troubler. موذي گري mūẕī-garī, s. f. Troublesomeness. موذيه mūẕīya, f. Noxious or hurtful (thing).

Bhūk maṅgāve bhik ko, bhūk hī sīs nivāë, Bhūk kahāve chorṭā, bhūk hī nāch nachāë . Rus. 'Tis hunger makes you beg, and hunger makes you bow your head, 'Tis hunger dubs you thief, and hunger makes you dance for bread. 2. To worry or harrass one; to lead one a pretty dance ( hairān k .). Yeh qavāīd nahīṅ hai, roṭī nāch nachā rahī hai . This is not drill, it is the belly's dance. ghoṛoṅ kā nāch , An exhibition of artificial horses. lauṅḍoṅ kā nāch , n. m. An exhibition of dancing boys ( bhagat , 4).
s. m. Dance. ناچ نچانا nāch nachānā, To make to dance; to tease.

def: s.m. vulg. corr. of nakshatra, q.v. s.m. A month computed by the moon's passage through the twenty-seven mansions, or a month of thirty days of sixty ghaṛīs each.
more: n. m. S. नक्षत्र Pālī, nakkhattam . A lunar mansion or constellation in the moon's path; an asterism. Besides the common division of the Zodiac into twelve signs, the Hindus divide it into twenty-seven Nachhatras , two and a quarter of which are included in each sign. 1. Tape nakhat mirgsīrā jo tab barkhā pūran jag ho . A hot tenth luner mausion, good rains on all the earth. 2. Jāne kis nichhattar meṅ paidā huā hai? (Said of a wicked boy). Who knows in what lunar mansion this imp was born? nichhat'tarī , adj. Born under a lucky planet; fortunate.

s. m. 1. A lunar mansion or constellation in the moonʼs path, a star or asterism. (Besides the common division of the zodiack into twelve signs, the Hindūs divide it into twenty-seven Nachh̤atras, each of which has its appropriate name, and two and a quarter of which are included in each sign).

def: s.m. Sounding; a sound (in general); singing; a song; a loud sound; roaring, a roar; noise, cry; — (in the yog) the nasal sound represented by a semicircle, and used as an abbreviation, or a hieroglyphic, in mystical words
subs: To sing; — to roar (as a tiger); to bellow

def: s.m. See نڙ naṛ.

ety: prob. S. नाद (rt. नद्)+क+क+इकः
def: s.m. A spout; a canal (cf. nadī).
more: s. m. A spout, a canal.

def: s.f. A stone brought by pilgrims from Mecca on which are inscribed benedictions or prayers from the Qorʼān, beginning with the words nādi-ʻalī (such stones are hung as charms round the necks of children).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَلِی (alī), from Arabic عَلِيّ (ʕaliyy).

def: s.f. corr. of the above, q.v.

ety: for A. ندامة, inf. n. of ندم 'to repent'
def: s.f. Repentance, penitence, contrition, regret; shame (syn. pashemānī).
more: (from ندم) s. f. Repentance, penitence, contrition, regret.

adj. & s.m. = P نادار nā-dār, adj. & s.m. See s.v. نا nā.

adj. & s.m. = P نادان nā-dān, adj. & s.m. See s.v. نا nā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نادار (nādār).

(adj. superl. of ندف Heb. ) s. m. A cotton-dresser.

ety: fr. naddāf

more: s. f. Dressing of cotton, the trade of a naddāf.

ety: fr. nadī

s.f. A species of water-reed, Calamus fasciculatus; — the plant Pranna herbacea; — Sesbania œgyptiaca; — the China rose.
s.f. The plant Premna herbacea.

ety: nādh˚ = S. नद्ध, p.p.p. of rt. नह्
def: v.t. To yoke; — to begin, to enter upon (also nāṅdhnā; nāṅdnā).
more: v. a. S. नद्ध bound. To yoke.

def: adj. (f. -inī), Sounding, resonant; sounding aloud.

ety: Prk. नदिआ(?); S. नदिका, rt. नद्; — S. also नदी
def: s.f. A river (the common personification of rivers being fem.)
more: n. f. S. नद् to roar. Pr. naī . A river. 1. ' Pratham pratham Brāhmane,' 'nadī nālābibarjite .' The first place is the Brahman's, bar streams and rivers. (The answer made by a Brahman who was asked to lead in a ford in virtue of the precedence he claimed). 2. Jo purubā puruā ke pāve, sukhlī nadiyā nāū chalāve . Bhoj. Prov. If from the Eastern constellation East winds blow In rivers dry of sailing boats there 'll be a show. 3. Dhīre baho, nadyā, saiyāṅ utreṅge pār! Wom. song. Flow gently, stream, my husband is to go across!
subs: s.m. The tree Terminalia (or Pentaptera) arjun
vulg. nadīj, adj. & s.m. (f. -jā), River-born, water-born, water-produced, aquatic; — any creature born in a river or in river-water; — the tree Terminalia arjun; — the marshy date-tree
vulg. nadīs (nadī+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of rivers,' the ocean

s.m. 'Lover of rivers,' the ocean; — the small tree Vitex negundo; — the tree Baringtonia acutangula; — nadī-kāntā, s.f. 'The beloved of rivers,' the rose-apple, Eugenia jambolana; — the shrub Leea hirta
s.m. (lit. 'fond of river-banks') A species of reed, Calamus rotang.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit नदी (nadī).

def: (for ناد عليّاً) Proclaim Aﻌlī or invoke Aﻌlī.
s.m. corr. of nandī-bail, q.v. (cf. nāṅdiyā).
unaware, ignorant, unseen
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نادیده (nādīda, “unseen”).

def: part. adj. & s.m. Unseen; — who has not (or never) seen; — greedy, famine-stricken, voracious; — eager, longing, wistful; — a hungry or greedy person, a glutton
more: adj. lit . unseen. 1. Greedy; famine-stricken. 2. Longing; wistful. Yeh dīde na-dīde haiṅ dīdār ke . Prov. The eyes are never tired of seeing. nadīdah , n. m. 1. A greedy person. 2. A glutton; gormandizer. nadīdoṅ kī tarah taknā , v. n. To cast greedy eyes on; to stare.
subs: To look or stare wistfully (at), to cast greedy eyes (on).

def: s.f. An Indian hour, or twenty-four minutes (or one-sixtieth of a sidereal day); — a piece of metal on which the hours are struck, an Indian clock, a gong; — a particular measure of length (equal to half a ḍanḍa).
(act. part. of ندم 'to repent,' &c.)., adj. Repenting; penitent, sorry (for); — bashful; ashamed, abashed.
more: (part. act. of ندم) adj. Bashful, penitent, repenting, abashed, ashamed.

ety: v. adj. fr. ندم 'to be a pot-companion,' &c.
def: s.m. Pot-companion, boon-companion; intimate friend, familiar, confidant; — courtier; privy counsellor; — a king's jester or fool.
more: (from ندم) s. m. A companion, intimate friend; courtier.

ety: act. part. of ندر 'to remain alone,' &c.
def: adj. Singular, rare, uncommon, unusual; wonderful; precious; exquisite; choice (cf. shāẕ-o-nādir)
more: adj. Rare; uncommon. Shāz o nādir. nādir-shāhī, nādir-gardī , n. f. Misrule; maladministration, as of Nadir Shah. nād'rī , n. f. The aee of cards. Har chahār nādrī qabl az aftāb . Card-players. Four aces are greater than the Sun-card.
(part. act. of ندر) adj. Rare, wonderful, exquisite, choice. (Plur. نادرات nādirāt, Rarities). نادر روزگار nādir-i-rozgār, The wonder of the world, unequalled.
subs: Name of a king of Persia (who reigned from A.D. 1736 to A.D. 1747)
adj. Of or relating to Nādir-shāh, or his conquests, or misrule; — s.f. The invasion of India by Nādir-shāh; — misrule as of Nādir-shāh
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نادِر, from Arabic نَادِر (nādir).

ety: for A. نادرة nādirat, fem. of nādir
def: adj. = nādir; — s.m. An uncommon thing, a rarity, curiosity; — an incomparable man, a rara avis.
s.f. pl. (of نادرةَ nādirat; see nādira), Uncommon things, rarities, curiosities.

ety: fr. nādir
def: s.f. Rarity; choiceness; — a rarity; curiosity; — (in cards) the ace.
more: s. f. A rarity, rarity, choiceness.

def: Relating to Nādir Shāh, the invasion of India by that prince.
direct plural of ندی
s.m. = نانديا nāṅdiyā, q.v. s.m. The bull and vehicle of Śiva
more: n. m. S. नन्दि The bull and vehicle of Siva.
subs: s.m. Idem; — a show-bull led about from door to door, and taught to obey commands, and to make certain answers, &c.

subs: s.m. The tree Terminalia (or Pentaptera) arjun

vulg. nadīs (nadī+īśa), s.m. 'Lord of rivers,' the ocean

s.m. 'Lover of rivers,' the ocean; — the small tree Vitex negundo; — the tree Baringtonia acutangula; — nadī-kāntā, s.f. 'The beloved of rivers,' the rose-apple, Eugenia jambolana; — the shrub Leea hirta
s.m. (lit. 'fond of river-banks') A species of reed, Calamus rotang.

vocative plural of ندی

more: n. m. Dim. of nāṅd . S. ननदा A small carthen pan.
s. m. A large earthen pan.

(dimin. of ناي) s. m. A small reed, a ḥuḳḳa snake.
Nigeria (a country in the West Africa, Africa)
wety: Borrowed from English Nigeria.

def: s. f. (dakh.) See نايکا nāyikā, sign. 2.

ety: Pehl. nāfak; Zend nabi; S. नाभि
def: s.f. The navel; — nave; middle, or centre (of anything)
more: n. f. S. नभि 1. See nābh . 2. Umbilicus. nāf-kā , adj. Umbilical. nāf ṭalnā, jānā, yā ḍignā , v. n. To be dislocated (the navel). nāf-i-shahar . The centre of a city.
s. f. The navel; the middle.
subs: or nāf jānā, or nāf ḍignā or ḍig-jānā (-kī), The muscles of the navel to be displaced or ruptured (through fifting a heavy weight, or other cause)
adj. (f. -kī), Of or pertaining to the navel, umbilical.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ناف (nāf). Doublet of نابھی (nābhi). Related to English navel.

ety: inf. n. of نفع 'to be useful, or profitable,' &c.
def: s.m. Gain, profit, advantage, emolument, proceeds, interest
more: n. m. Profit (فايده 1, 2). Har nām naphā, sab dund kathā . Prov. In Har's name profit lives, all other sounds but empty sound. nafa uṭhānā , v. n. See fāëdah uṭhānā , 1. nafa o nuqsān . Profit and loss.
s. m. Profit, gain, advantage, interest. نفع کرنا nafﻌ karnā, To profit, to benefit.
subs: (-se), or nafʻ karnā, To reap (or to make) profit or advantage (from, or by), to profit, to benefit
adj. Conferring benefits, beneficent; — yielding profit or advantage, profitable, advantageous; — nafʻa-o-nuqṣān, Profit and loss.

ety: rel. n. fr. nāf, q.v.
def: s.m. A bag or bladder of musk, musk-bag (i.q. nāfa-ě-mushk).
more: s. m. A pod of musk. (Also نافۀ مشک nāfa-e-mushk ).

ety: for A. نفقة nafaqat, fr. nafaq, inf. n. of نفق 'to perish,' &c.
def: s.m. The necessary expenses for living; expenditure, disbursement; — a maintenance.
more: s. m. The necessary expenses for living.

def: s.m. Plunder (esp. taken from infldels); spoil, prey.

def: s.m. A servant; a groom; — one person, an individual; one hand (e.g. tīn nafar, 'three persons, three hands; three')
more: n. m. One hand (a workman). Tin nafar . Three hands. naf'rī , n. f. Daily service or wages.
s. m. 1. A groom, a servant. 2. One person; as, تين نفر tīn nafar, Three persons. ¶ nafr, Fright, terrour.
subs: s.m. Settlement of land-revenue, or taxes, &c., made with each individual cultivator himself, independent of any landholder (i.q. raʻīyat-wārī faiṣala).

ety: from nafar
def: s.f. Service, daily work or business (such as a groom's, or the like); — daily wages; — a giant; a demon.
more: s. f. 1. Service, profession, trade (particularly, of a groom). 2. A giant, demon.

def: disobedient
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نافرمان (nāfarmān). By surface analysis, نَاـ (nā-) + فَرْمَان (farmān).

def: disobedience
wety: From نا (nā, “not”) + فرماں (farmāṉ, “order; command”) + بردار (bardār, “bearer”) + ی (ī).

ety: inf. n. of نفس 'to desire; to esteem,' &c.
def: s.m. Breath, respiration; — the voice or sound from the breast; — a moment, an instant (syn. dam)
more: (Heb. ) s. m. 1. The breath, respiration. 2. The voice or sound from the breast. (Plur. انفاس anfās ). نفس باز پسين nafas-i-bāz-pasīn, The last breath. ¶ nafs, s. m. 1. The soul, spirit, essence, substance, self. 2. Concupiscence, sensuality. 3. Sperm. 4. Desire. 5. Gravity, pride. 6. Envy. 7. Vice. 8. Penis. نفس الامر nafsu-l-amr, The essence, soul, or foundation of a thing, reality. نفس اماره يا بـﮩيمي nafs-i-ammāra or -bahīmī, Inordinate appetites, concupiscence. نفس پرست nafs-parast, adj. Sensual; sensualist. نفس سبعي يا لوامه nafs-i-sabʼīﻌ or -lauwāma, Irascibility or promptitude to the vindictive passions. نفس کشي nafs-kush-ī, s. f. Mortification of self, self-denial, temperance, penance. نفس مارنا nafs mārnā, To mortify the sensual appetites or self. نفس مطمينه يا ملکي nafs-i-mut̤maʼinna or -malakī, Benevolence.

ety: for A. نفاسة, inf. n. of نَفُسَ 'to be precious,' &c.
def: s.f. Preciousness, exquisiteness, choiceness; — purity; refinement.

ety: fr. naft̤, q.v.
def: s.m. Naphtha, bitumen, or a place where it is found in plenty; — lamp-oil.
more: (from نفط) s. m. 1. Naphtha, bitumen, or a place where it is found in plenty. 2. Lamp-oil.

ety: inf. n. of نفذ 'to penetrate, pervade,' &c.
def: s.m. Penetrating, pervading, piercing through; penetration; — going forth, being issued; passing, leaving behind; — escape (from an enemy); — adj. Arrived, reached (as a letter); — issued; obeyed (as an order).
more: (from نفذ) s. m. Penetrating, pervading, piercing; escape from an enemy. adj. Obeyed (an order).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic نَفَاذ (nafāḏ).

def: ordination
s. m. (dakh.) See نفع nafﻌ.

ety: act. part. of نفع 'to be useful, or beneficial,' &c.
def: adj. Useful; profitable, advantageous; beneficial, salutary, wholesome, good.
more: (part. act. of نفع) adj. Salutary, profitable, advantageous, useful.

ety: inf. n. of نفح 'to breathe or diffuse odour'
def: s.m. Breathing; blowing (the wind); diffusing (odour, &c.).

ety: for A. نافٍ nāfiṉ, act. part. of نفي 'to reject,' &c.
def: part. adj. Rejecting; denying; negative.

ety: for A. نَفْيٌ nafyuṉ, inf. n. of نَفَي 'to prohibit,' &c.
def: s.m. Forbidding, prohibiting; prohibition; — denying, disowning, disavowing; denial; — negation; (in Gram.) the negative imperative; — rejecting; refusing; rejection; refusal; — abandoning; driving out or away, expelling, banishing, exiling; — annihilation, non-existence; — (for A. نَهّي nafīyuṉ, v. adj.), adj. & s.m. Negative; — (in Logic) the negative attribute; — (in Alg.) minus; — what is rejected, or thrown away, &c.; refuse; filth; rubbish; froth; scum; dross
more: n. m. Negation; minus. nafī o isbāt . Negation and affirmation.
s. m. 1. Forbidding, prohibition. 2. Rejecting, refusing. 3. Negation. 4. Refuse, filth, refusal, annihilation, nonexistence.
subs: v.t. To forbid, prohibit; to deny; to negative; to refuse; to reject; to banish, &c.

def: s.m. = P نافلت nāfilat, s.f. (for A. نافلة nāfilat, fem. of nāfil, act. part. of نفل 'to present with an extraordinary gift,' &c.), A voluntary act of religion (the observance of which is not positively enjoined); a work of supererogation; — supererogatory prayers; — religious merit.

s.f. = P نافله nāfila, s.m. (for A. نافلة nāfilat, fem. of nāfil, act. part. of نفل 'to present with an extraordinary gift,' &c.), A voluntary act of religion (the observance of which is not positively enjoined); a work of supererogation; — supererogatory prayers; — religious merit.
s.f. A kind of trumpet (smaller than the karnā); — a clarionet; — a fife
s.f. A kind of trumpet (smaller than the karnā); — a clarionet; — a fife
more: (from نفير) s. f. A kind of trumpet.

ety: v. adj. fr. نَفُسَ 'to be precious,' &c.
def: adj. Precious; choice; exquisite; delicate; pure; refined.
more: adj. نفس breath, essence. Pure; exquisite; choice; refined. Nafīs khānā yā kapṛā .

ety: for A. نافية nāfiyat, fem. of nāfī
def: part. adj. Rejecting; denying; negative.

ety: act. part. of نفذ 'to pass through, to penetrate,' &c.
def: part. adj. Passing through, getting through; penetrating, reaching (to); — having effect, generally received or obeyed (an order, or edict, &c.); issued, passed; in force, valid, operative
more: adj. Issued; passed. nāfiz k. , v. a. To issue; pass (an order).
part. act. (of نفذ) Penetrating, passing, getting through, having effect, obeyed (an order).
subs: v.t. To cause to have effect; to pass, issue, put in force (an order)
(-par), To penetrate or reach (to); to have effect (on); — to be issued, &c.

ety: inf. n. of نفخ 'to blow, puff,' &c.
def: s.m. Blowing (with the mouth), puffing; — sounding, winding (a horn); — inspiring, breathing (into); inflation; — swelling; flatulence; — pride, vainglory.
more: (Heb. ) s. m. 1. Blowing (with the mouth), sounding (a horn, &c.). 2. Inspiring, breathing into, inflation. 3. Swelling.

ety: inf. n. of نفل 'to present with an extraordinary gift,' &c.
def: s.m. A voluntary act of religion, the observance of which is not prescribed; certain prayers, the reading of which is not compulsory; a work of supererogation.
more: n. m. Certain Mah. prayers the reading of which is not compulsory.
s. m. A voluntary act of devotion which may be omitted innocently as not being prescribed, a work of supererogation. ¶ nafal, Plunder (from infidels).

def: (inf. n. of نفر 'to be frightened and run away'), s.f.(?) Running away in fright; — fright, terror.

ety: A. nafar+S. अ+कः
def: s.m. A menial servant; a groom, stable-boy, and the like (a form expressive of contempt).
s.f. (dialec.) = nafrāʼī, q.v. s.f. Service in a menial position (as the of groom, &c.).

def: s.f. Service in a menial position (as the of groom, &c.).

def: s.f. A woman servant (in a menial position); — the wife of a groom, &c.
more: s. f. Fem. of نفر nafar, sign. 1. q. v.

def: to hate, to abhor, to detest
ex: مَیں تُم سے نَفْرَت کَرْتا ہُوں۔
ma͠i tum se nafrat kartā hū̃.
I hate you.

ety: for A. نفرة, inf. n. of un. fr. نفر 'to fly (from
def: s.f. Flight; terror; — (in Pers. and Urdū) abomination, detestation, horror, abhorrence, aversion, disgust (syn. ghin)
more: n. f. Disgust ( ghin ). nafrat-aṅgez ; H. ghināonā , Disgusting; loathsome. nafrat k., yā khānā , v. n. To be disgusted. nafrīn' , n. f. Curse; malediction.
s. f. 1. Flight, terrour. 2. Abomination, aversion, abhorrence, disgust.
subs: adj. Exciting aversion, &c.; horrible, detestable, disgusting, loathsome
(-se), To fly (from); — to shrink (from) in disgust; to feel aversion or disgust (for), be disgusted (with)
(-se), To feel disgust (for), be disgusted (by, or with).

def: direct plural of نَفْرَت (nafrat, “hate; hatred”)
oblique plural of نَفْرَت (nafrat, “hate; hatred”)

ety: imperat. or rt. of nafrīdan, contrac. of nā+āfarīdan; — nafrīn = nā, 'not'+āfrīn = Zend ā-frīna = S. आ+प्रीण; — or perhaps nafrīdan, in the sense 'to detest,' &c. = A. nafr+P. aff. īdan
def: s.f. Detestation, abhorrence; execration; — imprecation, curse, malediction; opprobrious words; reproaches; — contumely, ridicule; — terror, fright.
more: s. f. 1. Detestation, abhorrence. 2. An imprecation, curse, opprobrious words.

ety: v.n. fr. نفس 'to desire greatly,' &c.
def: s.m. (but f. in A.), Breath (of life), animal life; — soul; spirit, self, person; substance, essence, individual thing itself; a person, an individual; — mind, thought; will, pleasure, desire; — body; flesh; blood; — fact, truth, reality; — text (of a work); — concupiscence, carnal or inordinate desire, sensuality, lust, sexual passion; — sperm; — penis; — pride; grandeur, magnificence, pomp; — envy; vice, fault, blemish
more: n. m. Heb. nafach from fuch to breathe. 1. ( nafas ) The breath. 2. The body; person; self. 3. Essence; fact. 4. The sexual passion; lust. 5. The penis . 6. The text of a work. Nafs-i-kitāb. nafs-ul-amr , n. m. The essential truth or fact. nafs-ul-hāl yā amr , The facts or merits of a case. nafs-ul-matlab , The substance; purport. nafs-i-ammārā yā bahīmī , Sensual appetite; concupiscence. nafs-parast, nafsānī , adj. Sensual; carnal. nafs-parvarī , n. f. Selfishness. nafs-kushī , n. f. Self-mortification; selfdenial; penance. nafs mārnā , v. a. To mortify the body. nafsā nafsī . Every one for himself ( āpā āpī ).
subs: The essence, soul, or foundation of a thing; the essential truth or fact of the matter, the reality

s.f. Gratification of the appetites; — selfishness
The essence of the case; the facts or merits of the case

The essence of the matter, the substance, or real purport

ety: rel. n. fr. nafs
def: adj. Carnal, sensual; lewd, lustful; — voluptuous; luxurious.

ety: A. نفسانية, abst. s. fr. nafsānī
def: s.f. Animalism, carnality, sensuality; — luxury; — pomp, stateliness, pride, conceit; — egotism; self-opinionatedness; — passion, anger.
more: n. f. 1. ( nafs-parastī ) Sensuality; animalism. 2. Passion; anger. 3. Self-opinionatedness. 4. Pride; conceit. 5. Egotism.
s. f. 1. Luxury, sensuality, carnality. 2. Pride, pomp, stateliness.

ety: rel. n. fr. nafs
def: adj. Of or relating to the soul or self, &c.; carnal, sensual, animal.
more: adj. Of the soul, animal, carnal, sensual.

ety: Pehl. nift; Zend nap; Gr. νάφθα
def: s.f. Naphtha, bitumen.
wety: From Classical Persian نَفْت (naft), from Middle Persian [script needed] (npt' /⁠naft⁠/, “moist, damp; naphtha”), from Old Persian *naftah (“naphtha”).

def: s.f. The Ar. form of نفت naft, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of نفذ 'to penetrate,' &c.
def: s.m. Penetrating, pervading, piercing through (i.q. nafāz̤).

ety: Prk. नागो; S. नागः
def: s.m. A snake, serpent; the hooded snake, or cobra di capello, Coluber naga (which is regarded as sacred by the Hindūs); — a Nāga, or fabulous serpent-demon so called, having a human face with the tail of a serpent (the race of these beings is said to have sprung from Kadrū, wife of Kaśyapa, — or from Su-rasā, — in order to people Pātāla, one of the regions below the earth); — an elephant; — name of several plants, Mesua roxburghii (a small tree with fragrant blossoms); Rottlera tinctoria (used in dyeing); a species of grass, Cyperus pertenuis; Piper betel (i.q. nāg-bel); — one of the five vital airs of the body (that which is expelled by eructation); — poison; — a symbolical term for the number seven (or eight); — name of a district; and of a mountain; — (S. नागं) tin; lead; — one of the astronomical periods called Karaṇs; — adj. (f. -ī), Formed or consisting of serpents; serpentine; relating to serpents, or the serpent-demons; — elephantine; belonging to or resembling an elephant; produced from an elephant
more: n. m. 1. A snake; Coluber naja , Linn. 2. A demigod in the form of a serpent. nāg bhāshā , n. f. A Prakrit said to be used by the serpentine race who inhabit Pātāl (the lowest Hell). nāg-panchmī, nāg-pūjā , n. f. Hin. Wom. 1. Suakeworship. 2. A festival held on the fifth day of the dark fortnight of the month Bhādon. nāg-phāṅs , n. m. A noose used to seize an enemy in battle, resembling the arms of the Retiarii among the Roman gladiators. nāg-phanī , n. f. A prickly plant resembling the hood of a snake; cactus indicus. nāg-daun , n. m. S. नागदमनी Asparagus; asparagus officinalis . A kind of wood, by touching which it is said that fetters spontaneously fall off. nāg yā kālā nāg khilānā , v. a. lit . to cherish a snake. To put one's life in danger.

s. m. 1. A snake (Coluber naja) and a sort of demigod shaped as a serpent. ناگ بهاشا nāg-bhāsh̤ā, A Prākr̤it language said to be used by the serpentine race who inhabit Pātāl. ناگ پنچمي nāg-panchamī, Name of a Hindū holyday, the 5th of Srāwaṇ, sukl-paksh̤, on which day they worship a snake to procure blessings on their children. ناگ پاس ( नागपाश ) nāg-pās, or ناگ پهانس nāg-phāṅs, s. f. A running noose of a rope, &c. a sort of noose or knot, used in battle to entangle an enemy. ناگ کنيا nāg-kanyā, A race of females said to inhabit Pātāl, or the regions under the earth, and to be of serpentine extraction but very beautiful. ناگ لوک nāg-lok, The region under the earth, the abode of serpents and hydras, Pātāla. (Not being accessible to the sun, it is illuminated by very resplendent jewels). 2. An elephant. 3. Lead. 4. Poison.
subs: (˚ga+an˚), s.f. A graduated pole or post for showing the depth of water in a newly-dug pond; a boring-rod or stick driven into the earth
s.m. (lit. 'liked by elephants'), The holy fig-tree, Ficus religiosa
s.f. Name of one of the Prākrit dialects (the language of the Nāgas or serpent-race who inhabit Pātāla)
s.m. 'Elephant-destroyer,' a species of snake, Amphisbaena

s.f. Wife of a Nāga, or a female of the serpent-race (cf. nāg-kanyā)

s.f. Name of a Hindū festival, the fifth day in the light half of the month Śrāvaṇ (on which day they worship a snake to procure blessings on their children)
s.f. The prickly pear, Cactus indicus (a prickly plant resembling the hood of a snake)
s.m. Elephant's tooth or tusk, ivory
s.f. A species of sunflower, Heliotropium indicum

(S. nāga-damana), s.m. 'Snake-stick'; the plant asparagus (syn. mār-ćob); — a kind of wood, Staphylea emodit, by touching which it is said that fetters spontaneously fall off (sticks of it are supposed to keep off snakes, probably because the spotted bark resembles a snake's skin)
s.m. 'Enemy of the elephant,' an epithet of the lion
s.m. The orange (esp. the Silhet orange), Citrus aurantium
s.f. 'Serpent-virgin,' one of a race of very beautiful females, said to be of serpentine extraction and to inhabit Pātāla or the regions below the earth
(or kālā nāg) khilānā, 'To nourish a snake'; to put (one's) life in danger or jeopardy
vulg. nāg-kesar, s.m. A small tree with fragrant blossoms, Mesua roxburghii or ferrea (i.q. nāgesar)
s.f. The flower of the nāg-kesar

s.m. The world or abode of the serpent demons, one of the regions below the earth (otherwise called Pātāla, the abode of serpents and hydras; not being accessible to the sun, it is supposed to be illuminated by very resplendent jewels)
s.f. Mother of serpent-demons,' an epithet of Su-rasā; — red arsenic
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit नाग (nāga).

ety: Prk. नगो; S. नगः
def: s.m. Anything immovable; a mountain, a rock; a tree; — the stone of a ring, or a stone in which a name, &c. is cut; — a precious stone, gem, jewel; — a symbolical expression for the number seven (because of the seven principal mountains)
subs: (˚ga+āś˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Living in or frequenting mountains; — an animal or a creature that lives in or frequents mountains
s.m. 'Rock-splitter,' or 'tree splitter'; the plant Plectanthrus scutellaroides
adj. & s.m. Mountain-born; mountain; — a mountaineer
s.m. 'Chief of mountains,' the Himālaya range of mountains
vulg. nagaj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Mountain-born; mountain; — a mountaineer.

def: s.m. corr. of نخ naḵẖ, q.v. s.m. A thread (of yarn, or silk, &c.); silk thread (for flying a kite); — a kind of carpet with long pile; — rank, row, line (of soldiers, and the like); — a little; a few (cf. S. nakha).
more: n. m. 1. A mountain. 2. A precious stone ( nagīnah ). 3. A certain number. Aṅgūṭhī nag chār .
s. m. 1. A mountain. 2. The stone of a ring or a stone on which a name, &c. is cut, a jewel, gem, precious stone.

ety: Prk. नग्गओ; S. नग्नकः
def: s.m. A naked mendicant; a class of Hindū mendicants who go naked and carry arms (they sometimes serve as mercenaries to native princes); an individual of that class; — a barbarous tribe inhabiting the hills along the southern borders of Assam.
more: n. m. S. नाम. 1. A class of Hin. mendicants who go naked and carry arms. 2. Barbarous tribes inhabiting the hills along the southern borders of Assām.
s. m. A cast of Hindū faḳīrs.

ety: perhaps corr. fr. P. nā-gah
def: adj. Vacant, void; blank; nought; absent; unemployed; — s.m. A blank; absence; — intermission; adjournment, putting off, respite
more: adj. Vacant; blank; absent; nought. nāgah , n. m. 1. Absence. Do nāgah huiṅ . 2. Adjournment; intermission; respite. nāgah h., yā k . To be absent. bilānāgah , adv. Without fail; regularly; always.
1. adj. Vacant, void, nought, unemployed. 2. s. m. Adjournment, respite, putting off, intermission. ناغه کرنا nāg̠ẖa karnā, To adjourn, discontinue, intermit.
subs: v.t. To intermit; to adjourn, to discontinue; — to render blank or void (an entry, &c.); to be absent

def: s.m. A soft, sweet voice; — a musical sound or tone; — melody; song; modulation; trill, shake
more: s. m. 1. Melody, song, modulation. 2. A musical note. 3. A sweet voice. نغمهسراٴي nag̠ẖma-sarāʼī, s. f. Singing.

def: s.m. (in Pros.) A tribrach.

ety: Prk. नागिणी; S. नाग+इनी
def: s.f. A female serpent; — a female of the Nāga race; the wife of a Nāga or serpent-demon; — a female elephant; — (poet.) a ringlet
more: n. f. S. नागिनी, 1. A female serpent. ' Jo nāgan ghūṅgaṭ base, gorī, kaise jīve bhartār? 'Are lālā! oh to mere ghar kā gāheṛn pīve lahar ulār . 'If, fair one, 'neath your veil's a snake, how doth your husband do?' 'Snake-charmer, Sir, my spouse the poison draws, then sucks me through.' 2. ( Poet .) A ringlet ( zulf ). 3. ( līlī nāgan , Wom.) A quarrelsome woman ( utār , 3).
subs: s.f. A quarrelsome woman; a termagant.
wety: From ناگ (nāg) + ـاِن (-in).

s.m. = S ناگانچلا नागांचला nāgāṅćalā, s.f. See s.v. nāg.

s. (dakh.) A plough ( Harris ): see ناگل nāgal.

def: s.f. = nāgarī, q.v. s.f. (fr. nāgar), The Nāgarī (or Deva-nāgarī, the most common Hindī character of writing); — city dialect (as compared with the dialect of the village); — a clever, or crafty, or intriguing woman; — a species of Euphorbia.
s.m. A city, town
more: n. m. Dim. nagrī, naglā, naglī , n. f. S. नगर Pr. naaro . A town; city. Ek nagar ke doë bādshāh, do mil chhatr dhareṅ, Maiṅ toë pūchhūṅ, he sakhi, kis bidh rāj kareṅ? Riddle. Ans. The breasts. One city and two kings, umbrellas both display, I ask you, dear, how can they both have regal sway? nagar-bāsī , n. m. A citizen; an inhabitant of a town. nagar-nārī , n. f. A woman of the town; a courtesan. nagar yā gāoṅ prabandh , The government of a town. nagar-nāyak , n. m. A chief among bards.
s. m. A city, a town. نگر ناري nagar-nārī, s. f. A courtezan.
